title: Probabilistic Generative Modeling Notes
text:
1 Preface
These are my notes on probabilistic generative modeling.
The goal is to organize ideas around diffusion models, normalizing flows, inverse problems, and applications in imaging, audio, and neuroscience.
This is not meant to be a finished textbook yet. It is a living set of research notes.

title: 3  Score Matching
text:
3.1 Motivation
Score matching learns the score function of a probability distribution.

title: 3  Score Matching
text:
3.2 Key idea
Instead of modeling the density directly, we model its gradient with respect to the data.

title: 3  Score Matching
text:
3.3 Notes

Score function
Denoising score matching
Connection to diffusion models

title: 4  DDPM
text:
4.1 Motivation
Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models generate samples by reversing a gradual noising process.

title: 4  DDPM
text:
4.2 Forward process
The forward process gradually adds Gaussian noise to data.

title: 4  DDPM
text:
4.3 Reverse process
The learned model removes noise step by step.

title: 5  EDM
text:
5.1 Motivation
EDM provides a clean design space for diffusion model sampling and training.

title: 5  EDM
text:
5.2 Key ideas

Noise parameterization
Preconditioning
Sampling schedule
Solver choice

title: 6  Flow Matching
text:
6.1 Motivation
Flow matching learns a continuous-time vector field that transports noise to data.

title: 6  Flow Matching
text:
6.2 Key idea
Instead of learning a score, the model learns a velocity field.

title: 6  Flow Matching
text:
6.3 Notes

Probability paths
Conditional flow matching
Relation to diffusion models
